options: It is essential for understanding the condition

s3.q: What is the recommended minimum time before marriage to conduct premarital screening?
s3.explain: Couples planning to marry are advised to conduct premarital screening at least three months before the marriage date.

s3.q: How long is the premarital compatibility certificate valid?
s3.explain: The premarital compatibility certificate is valid for only six months.

s3.q: Which of the following best defines premarital care?
options: Care provided during marriage to treat hereditary diseases
Specific service provided for couples before marriage to promote health of future parents and prevent health hazards
A legal requirement only for consanguineous marriages
s3.explain: Premarital care is the specific service provided for the couples before marriage, aiming to promote health of future parents, prevent health hazards, and have healthy future generations.

s4.q: Which of the following is a key concept?
options: It is essential for understanding the condition

s4.q: When assessing this aspect, the most important factor is:
s4.explain: Assessment requires integration of clinical, laboratory, and imaging findings.

s4.q: Which of the following is NOT a component of premarital care?
s4.explain: The three main components of premarital care are: (1) Premarital Assessment, (2) Premarital Counselling, and (3) Immunization (Rubella vaccination for females).

s4.q: Among offspring of consanguineous marriage, there is increased risk of all EXCEPT:
s4.explain: Among offspring of consanguineous marriage, there is increased prenatal morbidity and mortality together with increased incidence of congenital abnormalities and mental retardation.

s5.q: Which of the following is a routine obligatory investigation in premarital screening?
s5.explain: Routine obligatory investigations include CBC, Blood group (ABO & Rh), HPLC (Hb variants), Sickledex, G6PD, Ferritin, HBsAg, and HCV.

s5.q: A 25-year-old man is undergoing premarital screening. Which genetic disorders should be specifically asked about in his family history?
options: Cystic fibrosis, Huntington disease, Marfan syndrome
s5.explain: Family history should specifically ask about SCD, Thalassemia, G6PD, and congenital anomalies such as Marfan syndrome and deaf mutism.

s6.q: According to the premarital care algorithm, after reception and triage, what is the next step?
s6.explain: After reception/registration and triage (vitals, anthropometric measurements), the client proceeds to the Premarital Screening Clinic.

s6.q: In the premarital care algorithm, what are the three main branches evaluated at the screening clinic?
options: History, physical examination, and laboratory investigations
Genetic testing, ultrasound, and blood work
Social assessment, nutritional counselling, and exercise plan
s6.explain: The premarital screening clinic evaluates three branches: History taking (personal data, consanguinity, genetic disorders), Physical examination (vitals, BMI, systemic exam), and Laboratory investigations (CBC, HPLC, Sickledex, etc.).

s7.q: Which type of premarital counselling involves studying the genogram of both parents to discover probability of genetic hazards?
s7.explain: Genetic counselling involves studying the genogram of both parents to discover probability of genetic hazards to offspring such as SCD, thalassemia, Marfan syndrome, and deaf mutism.

s7.q: Reproductive health counselling in premarital care includes advice about all EXCEPT:
options: Precautions during pregnancy to avoid teratogenic hazards
Management of chronic diseases like diabetes
s7.explain: Reproductive health counselling covers advice about appropriate time of first pregnancy, importance of early antenatal care, precautions during pregnancy (avoid drugs & teratogenic hazards), and safe delivery. Management of chronic diseases falls under relevant health problem counselling.

s8.q: Which immunization is specifically recommended for females during premarital care?
s8.explain: Immunization of females against rubella is recommended, not less than 3 months before planning pregnancy to avoid congenital rubella syndrome.

s8.q: Why is rubella immunization recommended before pregnancy?
options: To avoid congenital rubella syndrome if the female acquires infection during pregnancy
To reduce the risk of miscarriage in the first trimester
s8.explain: Rubella immunization is recommended to avoid congenital rubella syndrome if the female acquires the infection during pregnancy.
